before_code: theorem primeIdealZetaSum_ge_log_minus_bounded : ... primeIdealZetaSum K univ s ≥ Real.log (1/(s-1)) - C
after_code: theorem log_minus_bounded_le_primeIdealZetaSum : ... Real.log (1/(s-1)) - C ≤ primeIdealZetaSum K univ s
description: Always use ≤, never ≥ (and < not >) in Lean statements and hypotheses. Orient every inequality with the smaller side on the LEFT, and name such lemmas a_le_b not b_ge_a (e.g. log_minus_bounded_le_primeIdealZetaSum, not primeIdealZetaSum_ge_log_minus_bounded). mathlib convention. /cleanup's style audit did NOT catch a >= in a theorem statement/hypothesis, so this teaching adds the rule for future cleanup passes. Math prose inside docstrings may keep >= where it reads naturally; the rule is about Lean code.
